pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh - CSV generated during task setup to this path
Code analysis of setup_task.sh (line 40) and export_result.sh (line 40, 53)
Direct execution of setup query against Chinook_Sqlite.sqlite - query returns exactly 6 columns: TrackName, AlbumTitle, ArtistName, Genre, DurationMs, UnitPrice
Direct execution of query shows WHERE clause filters for g.Name = 'Rock' and all 407 returned rows have Genre='Rock'
Direct execution of query shows WHERE clause filters for t.Milliseconds > 300000; actual minimum duration in results is 300355ms, all rows satisfy >= 300000

privileged_info_summary: The CSV file contains 407 rock music tracks from the Chinook sample database (publicly available from GitHub). All tracks have duration exceeding 300 seconds (minimum 300,355ms, maximum 1,612,329ms). The CSV has 6 columns: TrackName, AlbumTitle, ArtistName, Genre (all 'Rock'), DurationMs, and UnitPrice. Data originates from a real iTunes Library imported into the Chinook database, with media data being real and sales/customer data being auto-generated or manually created.
